description_long: This barn features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in overlapping shingle-like tiles, giving it a distinctive and rustic appearance. The roof extends well beyond the walls, providing deep eaves and additional weather protection. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, and the structure is elevated slightly above the ground on short piers. The front facade includes a small central staircase leading to a single, narrow entryway, above which is a hexagonal window. Both long sides of the barn have a small, diamond-shaped window set high in the gable, adding symmetry and ventilation. The overall design is compact and functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on practicality.
description: A compact wooden barn with a steep gable shingle roof, horizontal plank siding, and small geometric windows. The structure is elevated on piers and features a central entry with a staircase.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central staircase, narrow entryway, and hexagonal window above the door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, steeply pitched shingle roof and a high, diamond-shaped window in the gable.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back wall with horizontal planks and no visible entry or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with the same roof and diamond-shaped window arrangement.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with extended eaves and shingle cladding
attribute_door_style: single narrow hinged door with a small staircase
attribute_window_arrangement: one hexagonal window above the front door, one diamond-shaped window high on each long side
attribute_architectural_style: traditional rural barn with vernacular influences
